title: Run an experiment with cumba_experiment()
text: Use cumba_experiment() observed experiments actual irrigation schedule want CUMBA reproduce . function used model calibration yield/Brix observations. companion function cumba_scenario() meant -deficit-irrigation studies, irrigation triggered automatically based phase-specific water-stress thresholds.

title: Run an experiment with cumba_experiment()
text: cumba_experiment() requires: daily weather table Site, Tx, Tn, P, DATE either Lat (estimateRad = TRUE) Rad; parameter object param (bundled cumbaParameters works); irrigation_df ID, Site, YEAR, DATE, WVOL.

title: Run an experiment with cumba_experiment()
code: library(cumba) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  data(tomatoFoggia)  weather       <- tomatoFoggia$weather irrigation_df <- tomatoFoggia$irrigation production    <- tomatoFoggia$production  out <- cumba_experiment(   weather       = weather,   param         = cumbaParameters,   irrigation_df = irrigation_df,   estimateRad   = TRUE,   estimateET0   = TRUE,   fullOut       = FALSE )  head(out)

what: Inspect simulated yield and Brix at harvest
title: Run an experiment with cumba_experiment()
text: (site × year × experiment) ends cycle complete. last day experiment carries harvest values:
code: harvest <- out |>   group_by(site, year, experiment) |>   slice_tail(n = 1) |>   ungroup() |>   select(site, year, experiment, yield, brix)  harvest

title: Run an experiment with cumba_experiment()
text: tomatoFoggia$production provides observed yield_ref (t ha⁻¹) brix_ref (°Brix). Yields short output q ha⁻¹, divide 10:
code: cmp <- harvest |>   mutate(experiment = as.integer(experiment)) |>   inner_join(production, by = c("experiment" = "ID")) |>   mutate(yield_t = yield / 10)  ggplot(cmp, aes(yield_ref, yield_t)) +   geom_point() +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2) +   labs(x = "Observed yield (t ha-1)",        y = "Simulated yield (t ha-1)",        title = "cumba_experiment - yield calibration check")  ggplot(cmp, aes(brix_ref, brix)) +   geom_point() +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2) +   labs(x = "Observed Brix (°)",        y = "Simulated Brix (°)",        title = "cumba_experiment - Brix calibration check")

title: Run an experiment with cumba_experiment()
text: diagnostics calibration internal variables, pass fullOut = TRUE: access kc, etR, fIntAct, carbonStateAct, floweringStateIde, fruitWaterContentAct, etc. every day.
code: out_full <- cumba_experiment(   weather       = weather,   param         = cumbaParameters,   irrigation_df = irrigation_df,   fullOut       = TRUE )  names(out_full)

title: Run an experiment with cumba_experiment()
text: Cardinal temperatures param must satisfy Tmax > Topt > Tbase soil must satisfy FieldCapacity > WiltingPoint; otherwise function stops clear error. supply param tibble values (instead list lists), make sure required parameter names present — function checks start-. Run-time scales linearly number (site × year × ID) combinations: simulated day--day independently.

title: Getting started with cumba
text: CUMBA (Carbon Use Model yield Brix Assessment) daily time-step crop model processing tomato. Given daily weather irrigation schedule, computes: thermal time, phenology root growth, soil-water balance three layers, carbon accumulation modulated heat, cold water stress, fresh-fruit yield Brix harvest. package exposes two top-level entry points: cumba_experiment() — run model one observed experiments, using user-supplied irrigation_df. cumba_scenario() — run model deficit-irrigation scenario mode, irrigation automatically triggered simulated water-stress falls phase-specific threshold.
code: library(cumba)

title: Getting started with cumba
text: complete default parameter set bundled package: cumbaParameters named list lists fields value, min, max, description. cumba_experiment() cumba_scenario() accept format directly convert internally tibble values.
code: str(cumbaParameters, max.level = 1) #> List of 30 #>  $ Tbase                       :List of 4 #>  $ Topt                        :List of 4 #>  $ Tmax                        :List of 4 #>  $ Theat                       :List of 4 #>  $ Tcold                       :List of 4 #>  $ FIntMax                     :List of 4 #>  $ CycleLength                 :List of 4 #>  $ TransplantingLag            :List of 4 #>  $ FloweringLag                :List of 4 #>  $ HalfIntGrowth               :List of 4 #>  $ HalfIntSenescence           :List of 4 #>  $ InitialInt                  :List of 4 #>  $ RUE                         :List of 4 #>  $ KcIni                       :List of 4 #>  $ KcMax                       :List of 4 #>  $ RootIncrease                :List of 4 #>  $ RootDepthMax                :List of 4 #>  $ RootDepthInitial            :List of 4 #>  $ SoilWaterInitial            :List of 4 #>  $ FieldCapacity               :List of 4 #>  $ WiltingPoint                :List of 4 #>  $ DepletionFraction           :List of 4 #>  $ WaterStressSensitivity      :List of 4 #>  $ FloweringSlope              :List of 4 #>  $ FloweringMax                :List of 4 #>  $ k0                          :List of 4 #>  $ FruitWaterContentMin        :List of 4 #>  $ FruitWaterContentMax        :List of 4 #>  $ FruitWaterContentInc        :List of 4 #>  $ FruitWaterContentDecreaseMax:List of 4 cumbaParameters$Tbase #> $description #> [1] "Base temperature for growth and development" #>  #> $value #> [1] 10 #>  #> $min #> [1] 6 #>  #> $max #> [1] 13

title: Getting started with cumba
text: package ships real-world dataset processing-tomato field trials Foggia (Apulia, Italy):

title: Getting started with cumba
text: minimal call uses default parameters bundled weather irrigation data: output data.frame one row per (site, year, experiment, day--year). fullOut = FALSE get short output: Set fullOut = TRUE get every internal state variable (carbon, flowering, fruit-water content, Kc, stresses, etc.).
code: weather       <- tomatoFoggia$weather irrigation_df <- tomatoFoggia$irrigation  out <- cumba_experiment(   weather       = weather,   param         = cumbaParameters,   irrigation_df = irrigation_df,   estimateRad   = TRUE,   estimateET0   = TRUE,   fullOut       = FALSE )  head(out)

title: Getting started with cumba
text: See Run experiment (vignette("cumba-experiment")) complete experiment-mode walkthrough tomatoFoggia. See Run scenario (vignette("cumba-scenario")) deficit-irrigation mode. See Calibrate ET₀ radiation (vignette("estimate_et0_rad")) radiation/ET₀ workflow.

title: Run a deficit-irrigation scenario with cumba_scenario()
text: cumba_experiment() reproduces observed irrigation schedule, cumba_scenario() runs model automatic deficit-irrigation mode. Irrigation triggered , simultaneously: simulated water-stress index ws falls phase-specific threshold (waterStressLevel), least minimumTurn days elapsed since previous wetting. amount water applied refills soil field capacity rooted layers. function use -studies, irrigation strategy design, climate-impact assessments.

title: Run a deficit-irrigation scenario with cumba_scenario()
text: waterStressLevel minimumTurn named lists one entry per phenological phase: vegetative — transplanting flowering, reproductive — flowering mid-ripening, ripening — mid-ripening harvest. Typical defaults: Lower thresholds (e.g. 0.3) impose deficit; higher thresholds (e.g. 0.7) imply frequent watering.
code: library(cumba)  waterStressLevel <- list(vegetative = 0.5,                          reproductive = 0.5,                          ripening = 0.5)  minimumTurn <- list(vegetative = 4,                     reproductive = 4,                     ripening = 4)

title: Run a deficit-irrigation scenario with cumba_scenario()
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)  data(tomatoFoggia) weather <- tomatoFoggia$weather  out <- cumba_scenario(   weather          = weather,   param            = cumbaParameters,   estimateRad      = TRUE,   estimateET0      = TRUE,   transplantingDOY = 120,   waterStressLevel = waterStressLevel,   minimumTurn      = minimumTurn,   fullOut          = FALSE )  head(out)

title: Run a deficit-irrigation scenario with cumba_scenario()
text: Run full deficit scenario compare yield Brix harvest:
code: full_irr <- list(vegetative = 0.7, reproductive = 0.7, ripening = 0.6) deficit  <- list(vegetative = 0.4, reproductive = 0.4, ripening = 0.3)  run_strategy <- function(level, label) {   cumba_scenario(     weather          = weather,     param            = cumbaParameters,     waterStressLevel = level,     minimumTurn      = minimumTurn,     transplantingDOY = 120,     fullOut          = FALSE   ) |>     mutate(strategy = label) }  scen <- bind_rows(   run_strategy(full_irr, "Full irrigation"),   run_strategy(deficit,  "Deficit irrigation") )  harvest <- scen |>   group_by(strategy, site, year) |>   slice_tail(n = 1) |>   ungroup() |>   select(strategy, site, year, yield, brix)  ggplot(harvest, aes(strategy, yield, fill = strategy)) +   geom_boxplot() +   labs(y = "Yield (q ha-1)", x = NULL,        title = "Yield response to irrigation strategy") +   theme(legend.position = "none")  ggplot(harvest, aes(strategy, brix, fill = strategy)) +   geom_boxplot() +   labs(y = "Brix (°)", x = NULL,        title = "Brix response to irrigation strategy") +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Run a deficit-irrigation scenario with cumba_scenario()
code: ggplot(scen |> filter(year == min(year)),        aes(doy, swc, colour = strategy)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point(aes(y = irrigation), size = 0.6) +   labs(x = "DOY", y = "Soil water content (%) / Irrigation (mm)",        title = "Soil water dynamics under two strategies")

title: Run a deficit-irrigation scenario with cumba_scenario()
text: cumba_scenario() require irrigation_df: schedule generated internally stress thresholds. simulation window starts transplantingDOY ends 150 days later (typical processing-tomato cycle length). crop coefficient (Kc) computed exactly cumba_experiment(): follows kcIni full canopy, decreases linearly senescence based achieved maximum light interception. guarantees yield Brix predictions directly comparable experiment scenario runs.

title: Calibration of ET0 and Radiation for Tomato Irrigation with cumba
text: vignette demonstrates use cumba assist tomato farmers irrigation scheduling calibrating estimating reference evapotranspiration (ET₀) solar radiation (RAD) weather-station data. workflow follows three progressive steps: Calibration — estimate ET₀ RAD Hargreaves-style formulae embedded cumba_scenario()/cumba_experiment() compare observed values sub-period. Parameter averaging — derive robust mean coefficients across calibration sites/periods. Validation — apply averaged parameters independent datasets compare estimated vs observed ET₀ RAD. rely open-access weather data (NASA POWER local agromet stations) simple crop metadata (sowing/harvest dates tomato experiments).

title: Calibration of ET0 and Radiation for Tomato Irrigation with cumba
code: library(cumba) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(lubridate)

title: Calibration of ET0 and Radiation for Tomato Irrigation with cumba
text: package ships real-world weather dataset Foggia (processing-tomato area, Apulia, Italy). already contains daily Tx, Tn, P, Rad columns Site Lat required model. want reproduce calibration data, CSV columns shipped testFiles/weather_foggia.csv.
code: data(tomatoFoggia) weather <- tomatoFoggia$weather str(weather)

what: Step 1 — Run the model with internal ET₀/Rad estimation
title: Calibration of ET0 and Radiation for Tomato Irrigation with cumba
text: Set estimateRad = TRUE estimateET0 = TRUE CUMBA compute radiation ET₀ internally using temperature-based formulae:
code: out <- cumba_scenario(   weather     = weather,   param       = cumbaParameters,   estimateRad = TRUE,   estimateET0 = TRUE,   fullOut     = TRUE )  out_sim <- out |>   dplyr::select(year, doy, et0, radiation)  head(out_sim)

title: Calibration of ET0 and Radiation for Tomato Irrigation with cumba
text: tomatoFoggia$weather provides observed daily radiation Rad (MJ m⁻² d⁻¹). Joining simulated outputs gives calibration scatter plots:
code: obs <- weather |>   mutate(year = year(DATE), doy = yday(DATE)) |>   select(year, doy, Rad)  cmp <- out_sim |>   inner_join(obs, by = c("year", "doy"))  ggplot(cmp, aes(radiation, Rad)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = 2) +   xlim(0, 35) + ylim(0, 35) +   labs(x = "Simulated radiation (MJ m-2 d-1)",        y = "Observed radiation (MJ m-2 d-1)",        title = "Hargreaves-based radiation vs. measured")  ggplot(cmp, aes(doy)) +   stat_summary(geom = "ribbon", aes(y = radiation), alpha = 0.4) +   stat_summary(geom = "ribbon", aes(y = Rad), fill = "grey50", alpha = 0.4) +   labs(x = "DOY", y = "Radiation (MJ m-2 d-1)",        title = "Seasonal pattern (simulated vs observed)")

what: Step 3 — Validation on an independent year
title: Calibration of ET0 and Radiation for Tomato Irrigation with cumba
text: Hold one season compare:
code: cal_years <- sort(unique(cmp$year)) val_year  <- tail(cal_years, 1)  val <- cmp |> filter(year == val_year)  ggplot(val, aes(doy)) +   geom_line(aes(y = radiation, colour = "simulated")) +   geom_line(aes(y = Rad,       colour = "observed"))  +   labs(x = "DOY", y = "Radiation (MJ m-2 d-1)",        title = paste("Validation - year", val_year),        colour = NULL)

title: Calibration of ET0 and Radiation for Tomato Irrigation with cumba
text: weather station provides observed ET₀, set estimateET0 = FALSE add ET0 column weather. CUMBA use observed values directly, isolating radiation estimator ET₀ estimator calibration.

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {cumba: Carbon Use Model for Yield and Brix Assessment in Processing Tomato},   author = {Simone Bregaglio},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/tomatoModelling/cumba_R_package}, }

title: CUMBA
text: CUMBA (Carbon Use Model yield Brix Assessment) daily time-step crop model processing tomato. Given daily weather irrigation schedule, computes thermal time, phenology root growth, three-layer soil-water balance, carbon accumulation modulated heat / cold / water stress, finally predicts fresh-fruit yield Brix harvest. package exposes two top-level entry points: cumba_experiment() — run model observed experiments using user-supplied irrigation schedule (calibration mode). cumba_scenario() — run model deficit-irrigation scenario mode, irrigation automatically triggered phase-specific water-stress thresholds.

title: CUMBA
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("tomatoModelling/cumba_R_package")

title: CUMBA
code: library(cumba)  data(tomatoFoggia)  out <- cumba_experiment(   weather       = tomatoFoggia$weather,   param         = cumbaParameters,   irrigation_df = tomatoFoggia$irrigation,   estimateRad   = TRUE,   estimateET0   = TRUE,   fullOut       = FALSE )  head(out)

title: CUMBA
text: vignette("cumba-getting-started") — model overview minimal example. vignette("cumba-experiment") — running observed experiments. vignette("cumba-scenario") — deficit-irrigation scenarios. vignette("estimate_et0_rad") — ET0 / radiation calibration workflow.

text: dataset contains default model parameters used Cumba package. element corresponds model parameter includes description, current value, minimum, maximum.

title: Model Parameters for Cumba — cumbaParameters
code: cumbaParameters

title: Model Parameters for Cumba — cumbaParameters
text: named list entry list : description Text description parameter value default value parameter min minimum allowed value max maximum allowed value

title: Model Parameters for Cumba — cumbaParameters
code: names(cumbaParameters) #>  [1] "Tbase"                        "Topt"                         #>  [3] "Tmax"                         "Theat"                        #>  [5] "Tcold"                        "FIntMax"                      #>  [7] "CycleLength"                  "TransplantingLag"             #>  [9] "FloweringLag"                 "HalfIntGrowth"                #> [11] "HalfIntSenescence"            "InitialInt"                   #> [13] "RUE"                          "KcIni"                        #> [15] "KcMax"                        "RootIncrease"                 #> [17] "RootDepthMax"                 "RootDepthInitial"             #> [19] "SoilWaterInitial"             "FieldCapacity"                #> [21] "WiltingPoint"                 "DepletionFraction"            #> [23] "WaterStressSensitivity"       "FloweringSlope"               #> [25] "FloweringMax"                 "k0"                           #> [27] "FruitWaterContentMin"         "FruitWaterContentMax"         #> [29] "FruitWaterContentInc"         "FruitWaterContentDecreaseMax" cumbaParameters$Tbase$value #> [1] 10

title: CUMBA model — experiment runner — cumba_experiment
text: Runs daily time-step CUMBA simulation one sites/experiments, computing tomato yield Brix weather inputs irrigation options. function validates inputs, converts list--lists parameter object tibble numeric values needed, prints blue message indicating running *experiment* mode.

title: CUMBA model — experiment runner — cumba_experiment
code: cumba_experiment(   weather,   param,   estimateRad = T,   estimateET0 = T,   irrigation_df,   fullOut = F )

title: CUMBA model — experiment runner — cumba_experiment
text: weather data.frame (tibble) daily weather site least Site, Tx, Tn, P, DATE , based flags, either Lat (estimate radiation) Rad (provided radiation). estimateET0 = FALSE, ET0 column must present. param Model parameters. Either: named list element list fields     value, min, max, description; form     automatically converted tibble parameter values; tibble/data.frame already containing required parameter values. object must contain (names/columns) : Tbase, Topt, Tmax, Theat, Tcold, FIntMax, CycleLength, TransplantingLag, FloweringLag, HalfIntGrowth, HalfIntSenescence, InitialInt, RUE, KcIni, KcMax, RootIncrease, RootDepthMax, RootDepthInitial, FieldCapacity, WiltingPoint, DepletionFraction, FloweringSlope, FloweringMax, k0, FruitWaterContentMin, FruitWaterContentMax, FruitWaterContentInc, FruitWaterContentDecreaseMax. Cardinal temperatures validated Tmax > Topt > Tbase. estimateRad Logical. TRUE (default), solar radiation estimated temperature (Hargreaves) Lat must weather. FALSE, weather must include Rad. estimateET0 Logical. TRUE (default), reference evapotranspiration (ET0) estimated temperature (Hargreaves). FALSE, weather must include ET0 column. irrigation_df data.frame irrigation schedule experiment/site, containing columns ID, Site, YEAR, DATE, WVOL. fullOut Logical. FALSE (default) returns key outputs; TRUE returns internal variables.

title: CUMBA model — experiment runner — cumba_experiment
text: data.frame containing input weather plus   daily CUMBA outputs per site date; set columns depends   fullOut.

title: CUMBA model — experiment runner — cumba_experiment
text: start, function prints "CUMBA running experiment mode" blue (via crayon). checks required columns/elements present throws informative errors anything missing. Sites inferred unique values weather$Site. param supplied list--lists value field, converted internally tibble::as_tibble(lapply(param, `[[`, "value")).

title: CUMBA model — experiment runner — cumba_experiment
text: weather: must include columns Site, Tx, Tn, P, DATE. Depending flags: estimateRad = TRUE (default), column Lat required; estimateRad = FALSE, column Rad required; estimateET0 = FALSE, column ET0 required. irrigation\_df: must include columns ID, Site, YEAR, DATE, WVOL.

title: CUMBA model — experiment runner — cumba_experiment
code: # Minimal reproducible inputs (toy data) weather <- data.frame(   Site = "TestSite",   Tx   = c(30, 32),   Tn   = c(20, 21),   P    = c(0, 5),   DATE = as.Date(c("2025-06-01", "2025-06-02")),   Lat  = 40 )  irrigation_df <- data.frame(   ID = 1L,   Site = "TestSite",   YEAR = 2025L,   DATE = as.Date(c("2025-06-01", "2025-06-02")),   WVOL = c(5, 10) )  # Parameters as list-of-lists (auto-converted): param <- list(   Tbase = list(value = 8, min = 0, max = 15, description = "Base T"),   Topt  = list(value = 26, min = 15, max = 35, description = "Opt T"),   Tmax  = list(value = 40, min = 30, max = 50, description = "Max T"),   Theat = list(value = 35), Tcold = list(value = 5),   FIntMax = list(value = 1), CycleLength = list(value = 120),   TransplantingLag = list(value = 5), FloweringLag = list(value = 40),   HalfIntGrowth = list(value = 0.5), HalfIntSenescence = list(value = 0.8),   InitialInt = list(value = 0.05), RUE = list(value = 2.5),   KcIni = list(value = 0.6), KcMax = list(value = 1.15),   RootIncrease = list(value = 0.01), RootDepthMax = list(value = 1.0),   RootDepthInitial = list(value = 0.1), FieldCapacity = list(value = 0.30),   WiltingPoint = list(value = 0.12), DepletionFraction = list(value = 0.5),   FloweringSlope = list(value = 1), FloweringMax = list(value = 1),   k0 = list(value = 0.01),   FruitWaterContentMin = list(value = 0.90),   FruitWaterContentMax = list(value = 0.95),   FruitWaterContentInc = list(value = 0.001),   FruitWaterContentDecreaseMax = list(value = 0.002) )  # Run (uncomment when the function body is implemented): # result <- cumba_experiment(weather, param, irrigation_df = irrigation_df)

title: the CUMBA model — cumba_scenario
text: daily time step simulation model computes yield brix degree tomato crop function weather data irrigation options.

title: the CUMBA model — cumba_scenario
code: cumba_scenario(   weather,   param,   estimateRad = T,   estimateET0 = T,   transplantingDOY = 120,   waterStressLevel = 0.5,   minimumTurn = 4,   fullOut = F )

title: the CUMBA model — cumba_scenario
text: weather dataframe weather data must following columns...... param named list model parameters, element list fields 'value', 'min', 'max', 'description' estimateRad boolean value estimate solar radiation based temperature using Hargreaves model. Default 'true' (implying column Lat present weather df) 'false' 'weather' df must Rad column estimateET0 boolean value estimate reference evapotranspiration based temperature using Hargreaves model. Default 'true' waterStressLevel float corresponding threshold water stress trigger automatic irrigation. Default .5, needed deficitIrrigation 'true'. minimumTurn integer corresponding minimum number days elapsed previous irrigation event. Default 4, needed deficitIrrigation 'true'. fullOut boolean, FALSE main output variables saved (default), TRUE variables saved deficitIrrigation boolean value estimate irrigation requirements. Default 'false', implying irrigation_df provided.

title: the CUMBA model — cumba_scenario
text: dataframe containing weatherDf plus daily outputs cumba model

title: the CUMBA model — cumba_scenario
code: #' # Example weather dataframe weather <- data.frame(   Site = "TestSite",   Tx = c(30, 32, 31),   Tn = c(20, 21, 19),   P = c(0, 5, 2),   DATE = as.Date(c("2025-06-01", "2025-06-02", "2025-06-03")),   Lat = 40 )  # Minimal parameters: use default package data params <- lapply(cumbaParameters, function(p) p)  # copy default parameters  # Run the model in automatic irrigation (scenario) mode # result <- cumba_scenario( #   weather, #   params, #   estimateRad = TRUE, #   estimateET0 = TRUE, #   waterStressLevel = 0.5, #   minimumTurn = 4 # )

what: Tomato field trial data from Foggia (Italy) — tomatoFoggia
title: Tomato field trial data from Foggia (Italy) — tomatoFoggia
text: dataset containing weather, irrigation, management, production data processing tomato field trials conducted near Foggia (Apulia, Italy). dataset organized list four data frames.

title: Tomato field trial data from Foggia (Italy) — tomatoFoggia
code: tomatoFoggia

title: Tomato field trial data from Foggia (Italy) — tomatoFoggia
text: list four data frames: weather Daily weather data columns:     DATE (POSIXct), Tx (max temp, °C), Tn (min temp, °C),     P (precipitation, mm), Rad (radiation, MJ m-2 d-1),     RHx (max RH,      Date (Date), Site ("Foggia"), Lat (latitude, numeric). irrigation Irrigation records columns:     ID (plot id), DATE (POSIXct), WVOL (mm), YEAR,     CV (cultivar), TRANS (transplanting, POSIXct),     HARV (harvest, POSIXct), IRR_M (management, e.g. "50     Site ("Foggia"). management Plot identifiers treatments columns:     ID, YEAR, CV, TRANS (POSIXct), HARV (POSIXct), IRR_M. production Production quality data columns:     ID, yield_ref (t ha-1), yield_ref_sd (t ha-1),     brix_ref (°Brix), brix_ref_sd (°Brix).

title: Tomato field trial data from Foggia (Italy) — tomatoFoggia
text: Carucci et al. (2024) https://doi.org/10.1016/j.dib.2024.110225

title: Tomato field trial data from Foggia (Italy) — tomatoFoggia
